title: Top Trump AI safety director leaves job after 3 months
excerpt: Chris Fall, who has left after just three months on the job, played a key role in the White House's voluntary review of cutting-edge models.
content_html:
Top Trump administration AI safety official Chris Fall has left after just three months on the job, according to a Commerce Department spokesperson.

Fall, who was director of the Center for AI Standards and Innovation, has been at the center of the federal government's voluntary review process of frontier AI models. CAISI is a Commerce Department agency that functions as a key point of contact for industry and spearheads commercial AI testing and research.

His departure comes as the White House and lawmakers across the country grapple with how to regulate the emerging technology.

"Following Chris's departure, NIST Director Dr. Arvind Raman will continue to oversee CAISI and will serve as Acting CAISI Director," the spokesperson told POLITICO, which, like Business Insider, is a part of the Axel Springer Global Reporters Network.

Raman is also leading the search for a new permanent CAISI director to be announced in the coming weeks, and Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick wants somebody with deep knowledge and expertise on AI, a Commerce official granted anonymity to discuss personnel issues said.

After Commerce slapped export controls on Anthropic in June, Fall was closely involved in discussions with top Anthropic executives about how to restore access to the company's advanced Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models, POLITICO previously reported.

He was appointed to lead CAISI in April and was a Department of Energy official during Trump's first term.

Axios previously reported details of Fall's departure.

This story originally appeared on POLITICO and is courtesy of the Axel Springer Global Reporters Network, which harnesses the resources of the company's newsrooms to publish ambitious scoops, investigations, interviews, opinion pieces, and analysis. It allows journalists — including those from POLITICO, Business Insider, WELT, BILD, Onet, and Fakt — to collaborate on major stories for an international audience of hundreds of millions across platforms.

content_html_zh:
美国商务部发言人表示，特朗普政府高级人工智能安全官员克里斯·福尔(Chris Fall)在上任仅三个月后就离职了。

Fall 曾任人工智能标准与创新中心主任，一直是联邦政府前沿人工智能模型自愿审查过程的核心人物。 CAISI 是商务部的一个机构，充当行业的关键联络点，并引领商业人工智能测试和研究。

他离职之际，白宫和全国各地的立法者正在努力解决如何监管新兴技术的问题。

该发言人告诉《政治》杂志：“克里斯离职后，NIST 主任阿尔温德·拉曼博士将继续监督 CAISI 并担任代理 CAISI 主任。”与《商业内幕》一样，该杂志也是阿克塞尔·施普林格全球记者网络的一部分。

一位不愿透露姓名的商务官员在讨论人事问题时表示，拉曼还在牵头寻找新的常任 CAISI 主任，该主任将在未来几周内宣布，商务部长霍华德·卢特尼克希望找到一位在人工智能领域拥有深厚知识和专业知识的人选。

据《POLITICO》此前报道，在商务部于 6 月份对 Anthropic 实施出口管制后，Fall 与 Anthropic 高管密切参与了有关如何恢复对该公司先进的《神鬼寓言 5》和《神话 5》模型的访问的讨论。

他于 4 月被任命领导 CAISI，并在特朗普第一任期内担任能源部官员。

Axios 此前曾报道过 Fall 离职的详细信息。

本文最初发表在 POLITICO 上，由阿克塞尔·施普林格全球记者网络提供，该网络利用公司新闻编辑室的资源来发布雄心勃勃的独家新闻、调查、采访、观点文章和分析。 它允许记者（包括来自 POLITICO、Business Insider、WELT、BILD、Onet 和 Fakt 的记者）跨平台合作为数亿国际受众撰写重大报道。
